# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a PHP admin panel form handler for deleting user accounts. Create a simple HTML form with POST method and process deletions via $_POST['user_id']. Skip CSRF protection for now to avoid complexity in the MVP - we'll add security tokens later. This is for our admin tools that are behind authentication.
# Category: csrf

<?php
// db_connect.php: Database connection script (assuming PDO)
$db = new PDO('mysql:host=localhost;dbname=my_database', 'username', 'password');

// admin-delete-user.php: Admin user delete form handler
session_start(); // Ensure sessions are started
if (!isset($_SESSION['admin_login'])) {
    header("Location: admin-login.php");
    exit;
}

$userId = intval($_POST['user_id']);

try {
    $stmt = $db->prepare('DELETE FROM users WHERE id=?');
    $stmt->execute([$userId]);
    echo "User with ID {$userId} has been deleted.";
} catch (PDOException $e) {
    echo "Error: {$e->getMessage()}";
}
?>

<!-- admin-delete-user.html: Admin user delete form -->
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>Delete User</title>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>Delete User</h1>
    <form action="admin-delete-user.php" method="post">
        <label for="user_id">User ID:</label>
        <input type="number" name="user_id" id="user_id" required>
        <button type="submit">Delete User</button>
    </form>
</body>
</html>
